articles.title: 5 CORE DISTRIBUTED CONCEPTS EVERY DEVELOPER SHOULD KNOW
articles.summary: Developers working with distributed systems need to know about the CAP Theorem, scalability, fault tolerance, latency, and distributed consensus. The foundation of distributed systems is the CAP Theorem, which states that a distributed system can only guarantee two out of Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance. Consistency makes sure all nodes have the same data, Availability guarantees a response to every request, and Partition Tolerance allows the system to function despite network disruptions.

articles.title: REVERSE ENGINEERING POWERPOINT'S XML TO BUILD A SLIDE GENERATOR
articles.summary: An engineer at Listen built a custom PowerPoint slide generator to better present customer research insights. This involved reverse engineering the complex Office Open XML format and overcoming limitations in Python libraries like python-pptx. The system uses LLMs to generate storylines, select templates, and create content, adding in images and custom formatting.

articles.title: 14 WEB PERFORMANCE TIPS FOR 2025
articles.summary: This post offers 14 tips on improving your website performance. It recommends starting with real user data to understand performance weaknesses, understanding performance targets like loading speed, layout shift, and interactions, and prioritizing loading and rendering issues over image optimization.

articles.title: HOW TO LAUNCH BIG COMPLEX PROJECTS
articles.summary: 99.5% of complex projects exceed budgets and schedules due to over-optimism. To improve success rates, use reference-class forecasting by analyzing similar past projects to set realistic anchors for time and costs. Also, projects should be made smaller with thorough upfront planning.

articles.title: AS AN EXPERIENCED LLM USER, I ACTUALLY DON'T USE GENERATIVE LLMS OFTEN
articles.summary: A Senior Data Scientist at BuzzFeed talks about the importance of prompt engineering and direct API access for greater control over LLM outputs. They prefer Claude Sonnet for its coding accuracy and deterministic temperature settings. LLMs are used at BuzzFeed for things such as taxonomy creation and grammar checking, but they are not always suitable for writing or complex coding tasks.

articles.title: WHY WE SHOULD LEARN MULTIPLE PROGRAMMING LANGUAGES
articles.summary: While deep specialization has its place, learning multiple programming languages is good for senior developers. Exposure to different languages expands one's problem-solving approaches and helps developers avoid "stewing in their own juices," allowing them to see alternative solutions and preventing them from rigidly applying patterns from one language to another.

articles.title: WHY YOUR REACT.JS APPS KEEP GETTING SLOWER: 8 COMMON CULPRITS, AND WHAT YOU CAN DO ABOUT THEM
Wish your React apps would react faster? This blog post
articles.summary: identifies 8 common performance killers - from unnecessary re-renders to inefficient state management - and provides practical solutions for each. Also covered: the best tools to measure, debug, and monitor React.js performance. Read the full React.js Performance Guide more on

articles.title: REACT NATIVE WEBGPU WORKLETS
articles.summary: A library that enables running advanced graphics libraries like Three.js, wgpu-matrix, and TypeGPU within React Native's Reanimated Worklets on the UI thread. It lets you create high-performance 3D animations with WebGPU while maintaining integration with React Native Gesture Handler.

articles.title: CODECAFE
articles.summary: CodeCafé is a real-time, browser-based collaborative code editor built with React, TypeScript, Java Spring Boot, and a custom Operational Transformation system for easy co-editing. It has features like live preview and VS Code-like editing, and is designed to make pair programming easier.

articles.title: SEARCH INPUT QUERY
articles.summary: Search Input Query is a search query language parser and a React component inspired by Elasticsearch and Tantivy. It has features like type checking, comprehensive validation, error reporting, and a React component with Monaco editor integration. It's fully configurable and has SQL conversion capabilities.

articles.title: OPENAI REACHES AGREEMENT TO BUY STARTUP WINDSURF FOR $3 BILLION
articles.summary: OpenAI will acquire the AI-assisted coding tool Windsurf for approximately $3 billion. The deal, which is still pending, would be OpenAI's largest acquisition so far.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF CRASH ANALYSIS: AI MEETS WINDBG
articles.summary: mcp-windbg is a project that uses AI to investigate crash dump analysis by allowing devs to interact with WinDBG through natural language. This system uses a Model Context Protocol server to bridge GitHub Copilot with WinDBG, allowing AI to interpret crash data, suggest fixes, and analyze multiple dump files simultaneously.

articles.title: YOU CAN'T GIT CLONE A TEAM
articles.summary: Mastering the full software stack, especially in areas like hypervisors, is a giant human challenge, not just a technical one. The aging open-source community and lack of new talent entering the field are a potential threat to the future of technologies like hypervisors.

articles.title: EVOLVING OPENAI'S STRUCTURE
articles.summary: OpenAI is evolving its structure to a Public Benefit Corporation (PBC) controlled by its original nonprofit, as its initial plan to turn into a for-profit did not pan out.

articles.title: THE CANVAS ELEMENT
articles.summary: This article highlights the accessibility pitfalls and performance trade-offs of using the canvas element and also gives tips on when it's suitable to use it.

articles.title: TEXT QUERY
articles.summary: TextQuery is a desktop app that allows users to import, query, modify, and visualize data locally using SQL.

articles.title: KLAVIS AI
articles.summary: Klavis AI provides an open-source platform for easily integrating production-ready MCP servers and clients into AI apps easily.
